data.body:
**Completion verification ‚Äî PASS**

All five acceptance criteria are met by the current implementation.

---

**AC1 ‚Äî Properties panel renders structured fields per step kind (no raw JSON textarea for the common path).**
MET. `rebarProvider.jsx` line 62 defines `STRUCTURED_KINDS = ["scripted", "agent", "loop", "map"]`. Line 616‚Äì621: when a known kind is detected, `structuredEntries(element, kind)` provides typed fields and `AdvancedRawEntry` is added as a collapsible fallback ‚Äî not the primary UI. Unknown kinds (line 624‚Äì630) fall back to the raw JSON editor as the only editor. The raw textarea is not the common path for known kinds.

**AC2 ‚Äî Properties panel delivered HERE; story 4df6 carries NO properties-panel AC.**
MET. Story 4df6 (`roomy-spine-mirth`) explicitly excludes the properties panel: its description states "OUT: properties panel (S8), branch/connection UX (deferred S9), IR semantics." Its four ACs cover exclusively layout (no overlap), edge waypoints, expanded sub-processes, and the roundtrip-demo fixture ‚Äî zero overlap with the properties panel.

**AC3 ‚Äî A browser e2e exercises structured editing round-trip (edit a field ‚Üí IR updates ‚Üí reload preserves).**
MET. `tests/e2e/js/browser_structured.mjs` drives a real headless Chromium session: selects the loop SubProcess, fills `#bio-properties-panel-rebar-loop-max`, blurs to commit, reads back `rebar:Config`, asserts `max_iterations == 7`, then types a non-numeric value and asserts a field error without blob corruption. The test (`test_editor_browser.py::test_editor_structured_fields_roundtrip_error_and_raw_fallback`, lines 62‚Äì93) wires all three assertions (round-trip, error/no-loss, raw fallback) and asserts the final value persists into the IR file.

**AC4 ‚Äî Known step kinds (scripted/agent/branch/loop/map) get structured fields; UNKNOWN falls back to raw JSON.**
MET. The prior verification found `branch` absent from `STRUCTURED_KINDS` ‚Äî this was addressed in the follow-up commit. `WhenEntry` (lines 241‚Äì273) is an EDITABLE structured field for branch: it reads `cfg.when` and writes it back via `mutateConfig` (lines 258‚Äì262), making it a genuine structured field (not read-only). The `rebarGroup` function (lines 604‚Äì613) handles `branch` explicitly: it pushes `WhenEntry` then `RawConfigEntry`. All five named kinds (scripted/agent/branch/loop/map) thus get at least one structured field; truly unknown kinds get only `RawConfigEntry`.

**AC5 ‚Äî Each structured field's setValue writes back into rebar:Config via serializer and round-trips; a field-level invalid/empty entry surfaces an error, not silent loss.**
MET. `mutateConfig` (lines 123‚Äì127) is the shared write path for all structured entries: parse blob ‚Üí mutate slice ‚Üí re-serialize ‚Üí `updateModdleProperties`. Field-level validation is present across all entry types: `WithFieldEntry.validate` returns "Required" for empty required fields and delegates to `coerceTyped` for type errors (lines 321‚Äì325); `ConfigTextEntry.validate` returns "Required" for empty required fields (line 370); `ConfigNumberEntry.validate` returns "Must be an integer" for non-integer input (lines 406‚Äì412); `WhenEntry` uses `mutateConfig` with conditional clear/set. Every `setValue` guards on `err` and returns early without mutating the blob (lines 327‚Äì328, 371‚Äì372, 413‚Äì414) ‚Äî no silent loss.
